title: Interpret quadratic models
description: Given a quadratic function that models a relationship, rewrite the function to reveal properties of the relationship.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jesse tossed a paint brush off her roof. The height of the brush (in meters above the ground) $t$ seconds after Jesse tossed it is modeled by

$h(t)=-5t^2+5t+10$

Jesse wants to know when the brush will hit the ground.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many seconds after being thrown does the brush hit the ground?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] seconds
hints:
###Choosing a form

When the brush hits the ground, its height above the ground is $0$ meters. So we're looking for what values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the brush.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in factored form

The zeros of the function tell us which values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in factored form:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-5t^2+5t+10
\\\\
&=-5(t^2-t-2)
\\\\
&=-5\left(t+1\right)\left(t-2\right)
\end{align}$

###When does the brush hit the ground?

The factored form of the function reveals its zeros:

$\begin{align}
&0=-5(t+1)(t-2)
\\\\
&t+1=0\text{ or }t-2=0
\\\\
&\xcancel{t=-1} \text{ or }t=2
\end{align}$

A negative value for time doesn't make sense in this context, so the brush hits the ground at $t=2$ seconds.

###Answers

1) The factored form of the function reveals when the brush hits the ground: 

$h(t)=-5\left(t+1\right)\left(t-2\right)$

2) The brush hits the ground $2$ seconds after being thrown.

assessment_items.question_content:
Alicia watched a drone take off from a bridge. The height of the drone (in meters above the ground) $t$ minutes after takeoff is modeled by

$h(t)=-3t^2+12t+96$

Alicia wants to know when the drone will land on the ground.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many minutes after takeoff does the drone land on the ground?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] minutes
hints:
###Choosing a form

When the drone lands on the ground, its height above the ground is $0$ meters. So we're looking for what values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the drone.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in factored form

The zeros of the function tell us which values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in factored form:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-3t^2+12t+96
\\\\
&=-3(t^2-4t-32)
\\\\
&=-3\left(t+4\right)\left(t-8\right)
\end{align}$

###When does the drone land on the ground?

The factored form of the function reveals its zeros:

$\begin{align}
&0=-3\left(t+4\right)\left(t-8\right)
\\\\
&t+4=0\text{ or }t-8=0
\\\\
&\xcancel{t=-4} \text{ or }t=8
\end{align}$

A negative value for time doesn't make sense in this context, so the drone lands on the ground at $t=8$ minutes.

###Answers

1) The factored form of the function reveals when the drone lands on the ground:

$h(t)=-3\left(t+4\right)\left(t-8\right)$

2) The drone lands on the ground $8$ minutes after takeoff.

assessment_items.question_content:
Paige launched a ball using a catapult she built. The height of the ball (in meters above the ground) $t$ seconds after launch is modeled by

$h(t)=-5t^2+40t$

Paige wants to know when the ball will hit the ground.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many seconds after launch does the ball hit the ground?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] seconds
hints:
###Choosing a form

When the ball hits the ground, its height above the ground is $0$ meters. So we're looking for what values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the ball.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in factored form

The zeros of the function tell us which values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in factored form:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-5t^2+40t
\\\\
&=-5t(t-8)
\end{align}$

###When does the ball hit the ground?

The factored form of the function reveals its zeros:

$\begin{align}
&0=-5t(t-8)
\\\\
&-5t=0\text{ or }t-8=0
\\\\
&\xcancel{t=0} \text{ or }t=8
\end{align}$

The ball launches at $t=0$, so the ball hits the ground at $t=8$ seconds.

###Answers

1) The factored form of the function reveals when the ball hits the ground:

$h(t)=-5t(t-8)$

2) The ball hits the ground $8$ seconds after launch.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ron started a construction company. The net value of the company (in thousands of dollars) $t$ months after its creation is modeled by

$v(t)=2t^2-12t-14$

If a company's net value is $0$ dollars, the company is breaking even. Ron wants to know how many months it will take for his company to break even.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$v(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many months after its creation does the company break even?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints:
###Choosing a form

Ron's company is breaking even when its net value is zero. So we're looking for what values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the value of the company.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in factored form

The zeros of the function tell us which values of $t$ make the output of the function $0$, so let's rewrite $v(t)$ in factored form:

$\begin{align}
v(t)&=2t^2-12t-14
\\\\
&=2(t^2-6t-7)
\\\\
&=2\left(t+1\right)\left(t-7\right)
\end{align}$

###When does the company break even?

The factored form of the function reveals its zeros:

$\begin{align}
&0=2\left(t+1\right)\left(t-7\right)
\\\\
&t+1=0\text{ or }t-7=0
\\\\
&\xcancel{t=-1} \text{ or }t=7
\end{align}$

A negative value for time doesn't make sense in this context, so Ron's company is breaking even at $t=7$ months.

###Answers

1) The factored form of the function reveals when the company breaks even:

$v(t)=2\left(t+1\right)\left(t-7\right)$

2) The company breaks even $7$ months after creation.

assessment_items.question_content:
Amir throws a stone off of a bridge into a river. The stone's height (in meters above the water) $t$ seconds after Amir throws it is modeled by

$h(t)=-5t^2+20t+160$

Amir wants to know when the stone will reach its highest point.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many seconds after being thrown did the stone reach its highest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] seconds
hints:
###Choosing a form

The stone's highest point relates to the maximum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the stone.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its maximum height, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-4}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{4}$:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-5t^2+20t+160
\\\\
&=-5(t^2-4t)+160&&\text{Factor } -5 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=-5(t^2\blueD{-4}t+\goldD{4})+160\redD{+20}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=-5(t-2)^2+180&&\text{Factor and simplify}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###When did the stone reach its highest point?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a maximum for $h(t)$ since the leading coefficient $-5$ is negative.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $h(t)=-5(t-\maroonD{2})^2+\tealD{180}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{2},\tealD{180})$, and we know the stone reached its highest point at $t=\maroonD{2}$ seconds.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals when the stone reached its highest point:

$h(t)=-5\left(t-2\right)^2+180$

2) The stone reached its highest point $2$ seconds after being thrown.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suraj watched a hovercraft take off from a platform. The height of the hovercraft (in meters above the ground) $t$ minutes after takeoff is modeled by

$h(t)=-4t^2+8t+32$

Suraj wants to know when the hovercraft will reach its highest point.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many minutes after takeoff did the hovercraft reach its highest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] minutes
hints:
###Choosing a form

The hovercraft's highest point relates to the maximum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the hovercraft.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its maximum height, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-2}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{1}$:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-4t^2+8t+32
\\\\
&=-4(t^2-2t)+32&&\text{Factor } -4 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=-4(t^2\blueD{-2}t+\goldD{1})+32\redD{+4}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=-4(t-1)^2+36&&\text{Factor and simplify}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###When did the hovercraft reach its highest point?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a maximum for $h(t)$ since the leading coefficient $-4$ is negative.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $h(t)=-4(t-\maroonD{1})^2+\tealD{36}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{1},\tealD{36})$, and we know the hovercraft reached its highest point at $t=\maroonD{1}$ minute.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals when the hovercraft reached its highest point:

$h(t)=-4\left(t-1\right)^2+36$

2) The hovercraft reached its highest point $1$ minute after takeoff.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ingrid hit a golf ball. The height of the ball (in meters above the ground) $t$ seconds after being hit is modeled by

$h(t)=-5t^2+30t$

Ingrid wants to know when the ball reached its highest point.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many seconds after being hit does the ball reach its highest point?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] seconds
hints:
###Choosing a form

The ball’s highest point relates to the maximum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the ball.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its maximum height, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-6}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{9}$:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-5t^2+30t
\\\\
&=-5(t^2-6t)&&\text{Factor } -5 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=-5(t^2\blueD{-6}t+\goldD{9})\redD{+45}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=-5(t-3)^2+45&&\text{Factor}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###When does the ball reach its highest point?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a maximum for $h(t)$ since the leading coefficient $-5$ is negative.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $h(t)=-5(t-\maroonD{3})^2+\tealD{45}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{3},\tealD{45})$, and we know the ball reaches its highest point at $t=\maroonD{3}$ seconds.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals when the ball reaches its highest point:

$h(t)=-5\left(t-3\right)^2+45$

2) The ball reaches its highest point $3$ seconds after being hit.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tom started an entertainment company. The net value of the company (in thousands of dollars) $t$ months after its creation is modeled by

$v(t)=4t^2-24t-28$

Tom wants to know when his company will be at its lowest net value.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$v(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) How many months after its creation does the company reach its lowest net value?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] months
hints:
###Choosing a form

The company's lowest net value relates to the minimum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the net value of the company.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its minimum value, so let's rewrite $v(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-6}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{9}$:

$\begin{align}
v(t)&=4t^2-24t-28
\\\\
&=4(t^2-6t)-28&&\text{Factor } 4 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=4(t^2\blueD{-6}t+\goldD{9})-28\redD{-36}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=4(t-3)^2-64&&\text{Factor and simplify}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###When does the company reach its lowest net value?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a minimum for $v(t)$ since the leading coefficient $4$ is positive.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $v(t)=4(t-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-64}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{3},\tealD{-64})$, and we know the company reaches its lowest net value at $t=\maroonD{3}$ months.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals when the company reaches its lowest value:

$v(t)=4(t-3)^2-64$

2) The company reaches its lowest net value $3$ months after creation.

assessment_items.question_content:
A construction worker tosses a brick from a tall building. The brick's height (in meters above the ground) $t$ seconds after being thrown is modeled by

$h(t)=-5t^2+20t+105$

Suppose we want to know the height of the brick above the ground at its highest point.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) At its highest point, how far above the ground was the brick?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints:
###Choosing a form

The brick's highest point relates to the maximum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the brick.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its maximum height, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-4}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{4}$:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-5t^2+20t+105
\\\\
&=-5(t^2-4t)+105&&\text{Factor } -5 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=-5(t^2\blueD{-4}t+\goldD{4})+105\redD{+20}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=-5(t-2)^2+125&&\text{Factor and simplify}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How high is the highest point?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a maximum for $h(t)$ since the leading coefficient $-5$ is negative.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $h(t)=-5(t-\maroonD{2})^2+\tealD{125}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{2},\tealD{125})$, and we know the brick's highest point was $\tealD{125}$ meters above the ground.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals the highest point:

$h(t)=-5\left(t-2\right)^2+125$

2) At its highest point, the brick was $125$ meters above the ground.

assessment_items.question_content:
A flying squirrel jumped from a tree branch and floated to the ground. The squirrel's height (in meters above the ground) $t$ seconds after jumping is modeled by

$h(t)=-2t^2+4t+30$

Suppose we want to know the height of the squirrel above the ground at its highest point.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) At its highest point, how far above the ground was the squirrel?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints:
###Choosing a form

The squirrel's highest point relates to the maximum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the squirrel.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its maximum height, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-2}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{1}$:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-2t^2+4t+30
\\\\
&=-2(t^2-2t)+30&&\text{Factor } -2 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=-2(t^2\blueD{-2}t+\goldD{1})+30\redD{+2}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=-2(t-1)^2+32&&\text{Factor and simplify}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How high is the highest point?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a maximum for $h(t)$ since the leading coefficient $-2$ is negative.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $h(t)=-2(t-\maroonD{1})^2+\tealD{32}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{1},\tealD{32})$, and we know the squirrel's highest point was $\tealD{32}$ meters above the ground.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals the highest point:

$h(t)=-2\left(t-1\right)^2+32$

2) At its highest point, the squirrel was $32$ meters above the ground.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sara kicked a football. The height of the ball (in meters above the ground) $t$ seconds after Sara kicked it is modeled by

$h(t)=-5t^2+20t$

Sara wants to know the height of the ball above the ground at its highest point.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$h(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) At its highest point, how far above the ground was the ball?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints:
###Choosing a form

The ball’s highest point relates to the maximum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the height of the ball.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its maximum height, so let's rewrite $h(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-4}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{4}$:

$\begin{align}
h(t)&=-5t^2+20t
\\\\
&=-5(t^2-4t)&&\text{Factor } -5 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=-5(t^2\blueD{-2}t+\goldD{4})\redD{+20}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=-5(t-2)^2+20&&\text{Factor}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###How high is the highest point?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a maximum for $h(t)$ since the leading coefficient $-5$ is negative.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $h(t)=-5(t-\maroonD{2})^2+\tealD{20}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{2},\tealD{20})$, and we know the ball's highest point was $\tealD{20}$ meters above the ground.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals when the ball reaches its highest point:

$h(t)=-5\left(t-2\right)^2+20$

2) At its highest point, the ball was $20$ meters above the ground.

assessment_items.question_content:
Paul opened a bakery. The net value of the bakery (in thousands of dollars) $t$ months after its creation is modeled by

$v(t)=2t^2-12t-14$

Paul wants to know what his bakery's lowest net value will be.

**1) Rewrite the function in factored or vertex form, choosing the form that makes the answer to the question visible in the equation.**

$v(t)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]

**2) What is the bakery's lowest net value?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] thousand dollars
hints:
###Choosing a form

The bakery's lowest net value relates to the minimum of the function.

Which form reveals this feature?

Here's a summary of what each form reveals along with examples. Note that these are all equivalent forms of the same function, but not the function modeling the net value of the bakery.

Form | Example | Feature revealed
- | - | -
Standard | $f(x)=2x^2-12x+\greenD{10}$ | $y$-intercept is $\greenD{10}$
Factored | $f(x)=2(x-\purpleC{1})(x-\purpleC{5})$ | Zeros are $x=\purpleC1$ and $x=\purpleC5$
Vertex | $f(x)=2(x-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-8}$ | Vertex is $(\maroonD3,\tealD{-8})$

###Rewrite in vertex form

The vertex of the function tells us the value of $t$ where the function reaches its minimum value, so let's rewrite $v(t)$ in vertex form by completing the square.

The number that will help us complete the square is $\left(\dfrac{\blueD{-6}}{2}\right)^2=\goldD{9}$:

$\begin{align}
v(t)&=2t^2-12t-14
\\\\
&=2(t^2-6t)-14&&\text{Factor } 2 \text{ from first two terms}.
\\\\
&=2(t^2\blueD{-6}t+\goldD{9})-14\redD{-18}&&\text{Complete the square}.
\\\\
&=2(t-3)^2-32&&\text{Factor and simplify}.
\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

###What is the bakery's lowest net value?

The vertex form of the function reveals its vertex, and we know this point is a minimum for $v(t)$ since the leading coefficient $2$ is positive.

In general, for any quadratic function written in vertex form $f(x)=a(x-\maroonD{h})^2+\tealD{k}$, we can conclude that the vertex is the point $(\maroonD{h},\tealD{k})$.

So for $v(t)=2(t-\maroonD{3})^2\tealD{-32}$, the vertex is $(\maroonD{3},\tealD{-32})$, and we know the bakery's lowest net value is $\tealD{-32}$ thousand dollars.

###Answers

1) The vertex form of the function reveals the bakery's lowest net value:

$v(t)=2\left(t-3\right)^2-32$

2) The bakery's lowest net value is $-32$ thousand dollars.
